name: uliplot

metadata.name: UliPlot
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Utilities for convenient plotting using matplotlib and other pydata libraries

documentation:
# UliPlot
After UliEngineering and UliAcceleration, this library in the Uli series provides convient functions for plotting using matplotlib and other pydata libraries

This library is currently in its Alpha stage and the API is not considered stable

### How to install

```sh
pip3 install git+https://github.com/ulikoehler/UliPlot.git
```

### How to use

```python
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt
import UliPlot as upl
```

Blog posts with specific topics on how to use UliPlot:
- [How to auto-fit Pandas pd.to_excel() XLSX column width](https://techoverflow.net/2021/03/05/how-to-auto-fit-pandas-pd-to_excel-xlsx-column-width/)

functions.name: UliPlot.XLSX.text_length
documentation: Get the effective text length in characters, taking into account newlines

functions.name: UliPlot.XLSX._to_str_for_length
documentation: Like str() but rounds decimals to predefined length

functions.name: UliPlot.XLSX.auto_adjust_xlsx_column_width
documentation:
Auto adjust column width to fit content in a XLSX exported from a pandas DataFrame.

How to use:
```
with pd.ExcelWriter(filename) as writer:
    df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name="MySheet")
    auto_adjust_column_width_index(df, writer, sheet_name="MySheet", margin=3)
```

:param DataFrame df: The DataFrame used to export the XLSX.
:param pd.ExcelWriter writer: The pandas exporter with engine="xlsxwriter"
:param str sheet_name: The name of the sheet
:param int margin: How many extra space (beyond the maximum size of the string)
:param int length_factor: The factor to apply to the character length to obtain the column width.
:param int decimals: The number of decimal places to assume for floats: Should be the same as the number of decimals displayed in the XLSX
:param bool index: Whether the DataFrame's index is inserted as a separate column (if index=False in df.to_xlsx() set index=False here!)

modules.name: UliPlot.Ticks
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: UliPlot.Ticks.set_num_xticks
documentation:
For the X axis, set a constant number of ticks, computing the
tick spacing in <nticks> equidistant steps from start to stop.

functions.name: UliPlot.Ticks.set_num_yticks
documentation:
For the Y axis, set a constant number of ticks, computing the
tick spacing in <nticks> equidistant steps from start to stop.

modules.name: UliPlot.TickFormat
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_axis_tick_format_func
documentation:
Set the tick formatter for an X or Y axis to set_axis_tick_format_func
Typically, this is not called directly.
Usually you should use set_xaxis_tick_format_func() or set_yaxis_tick_format_func()
instead.

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_yaxis_tick_format_func
documentation:
For the current Y axis, set <func(value, index)> as the (major & minor) tick formatter.
This is often used with lambda functions.

Example usage:

:param Function func: The formatter function (e.g. lambda value, index: ...)
:param Axis ax: You can specify an ax object here. If None, plt.gca() is used.
:param bool set_major: If True, set the formatter as major tick formatter
:param bool set_minor: If True, set the formatter as minor tick formatter

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_xaxis_tick_format_func
documentation:
For the current Y axis, set <func(value, index)> as the (major & minor) tick formatter.
This is often used with lambda functions.

Example usage:

:param Function func: The formatter function (e.g. lambda value, index: ...)
:param Axis ax: You can specify an ax object here. If None, plt.gca() is used.
:param bool set_major: If True, set the formatter as major tick formatter
:param bool set_minor: If True, set the formatter as minor tick formatter

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_axis_tick_format_float
documentation:
Set the number of decimals and the unit for formatting the given X or Y axis.
Typically, this is not called directly.
Usually you should use set_xaxis_tick_format_float() or set_yaxis_tick_format_float()
instead.

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_axis_tick_format_si_unit
documentation:
Use UliEngineering format_value() to format the axis ticks with SI units,
such as "mV" or "uA".

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_yaxis_tick_format_si_unit
documentation: Use UliEngineering format_value() to format the Y axis ticks with SI units,

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_xaxis_tick_format_si_unit
documentation: Use UliEngineering format_value() to format the X axis ticks with SI units,

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_yaxis_tick_format_float
documentation: Set the number of decimals and the unit for formatting the Y axis.

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_xaxis_tick_format_float
documentation: Set the number of decimals and the unit for formatting the Y axis.

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_axis_tick_format_datetime
documentation:
Set the date format for formatting the given X or Y axis.
Typically, this is not called directly.
Usually you should use set_xaxis_tick_format_datetime() or set_yaxis_tick_format_datetime()
instead.

```
param axis

The axis (X or Y) to set the formatter for

param str dateformat

The strftime date format string, e.g. "%Y%m%d %H%M%S"

param datetime.tzinfo tz

The time zone for the ticks, uses matplotlib default if None

param bool set_major

If True, set the formatter as major tick formatter

param bool set_minor

If True, set the formatter as minor tick formatter
```

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_yaxis_tick_format_datetime
documentation:
Configure the Y axis with datetime format ticks

:param str dateformat: The strftime date format string, e.g. "%Y%m%d %H%M%S"
:param datetime.tzinfo tz: The time zone for the ticks, uses matplotlib default if None
:param Axis ax: You can specify an ax object here. If None, plt.gca() is used.
:param bool set_major: If True, set the formatter as major tick formatter
:param bool set_minor: If True, set the formatter as minor tick formatter

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_xaxis_tick_format_datetime
documentation:
Configure the X axis with datetime format ticks

:param str dateformat: The strftime date format string, e.g. "%Y%m%d %H%M%S"
:param datetime.tzinfo tz: The time zone for the ticks, uses matplotlib default if None
:param Axis ax: You can specify an ax object here. If None, plt.gca() is used.
:param bool set_major: If True, set the formatter as major tick formatter
:param bool set_minor: If True, set the formatter as minor tick formatter

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_axis_tick_format_percent
documentation:
Set the percent format for formatting the given X or Y axis.
Typically, this is not called directly.
Usually you should use set_xaxis_tick_format_percent() or set_yaxis_tick_format_percent()
instead.

```
param axis

The axis (X or Y) to set the formatter for

param fullscale

What value is considered to be 100%. Use 100.0 for values that are already scaled as percent.

param bool set_major

If True, set the formatter as major tick formatter

param bool set_minor

If True, set the formatter as minor tick formatter
```

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_yaxis_tick_format_percent
documentation:
Set the number of decimals and the unit for formatting the Y axis.

```
param fullscale

What value is considered to be 100%. Use 100.0 for values that are already scaled as percent.

param Axis ax

You can specify an ax object here. If None, plt.gca() is used.

param bool set_major

If True, set the formatter as major tick formatter

param bool set_minor

If True, set the formatter as minor tick formatter
```

functions.name: UliPlot.TickFormat.set_xaxis_tick_format_percent
documentation:
Set the number of decimals and the unit for formatting the X axis.

```
param fullscale

What value is considered to be 100%. Use 100.0 for values that are already scaled as percent.

param Axis ax

You can specify an ax object here. If None, plt.gca() is used.

param bool set_major

If True, set the formatter as major tick formatter

param bool set_minor

If True, set the formatter as minor tick formatter
```
